behavior_summary: U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch is a process that records that the player has 'seen' a teleporter by setting per-location saw...Teleporter global flags based on the instance's map id.

The routine queries the map id of the teleporter instance (via Npc::getMap) and sets one or more saw...Teleporter flags for known map ids (Central Tenebrae, Plateau, Upper Catacombs, Mountain King, Argentrock Isle, Carthax Lake, Daemon's Crag).

If the process instance appears active, it attempts to initialize/activate a TELEPAD (inferred: invokes/spawns TELEPAD::func0080 on a telepad object) and then suspends itself — likely to let the telepad process run independently.
notable_control_flow: U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch begins by calling Npc::getMap(this) and branches on the returned map id to select which saw...Teleporter flag(s) to set.

Specific map id checks: 0x28 -> sawCentralTenebraeTeleporter, 0x08 -> sawPlateauTeleporter, 0x32 -> sawUpperCatacombsTeleporter, 0x1F -> sawMountainKingTeleporter, 0x25 -> sawArgentrockIsleTeleporter, 0x10 or 0x1A -> sawCarthaxLakeTeleporter, 0x0C -> sawDaemonsCragTeleporter.

A conditional path (when the instance is active/in context) invokes/spawns TELEPAD::func0080 (on an inferred TELEPAD object stored in local2) and then issues suspend to yield the U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch process.
open_questions: Exactly when U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch is scheduled/triggered in gameplay (on entity creation, on player proximity/enterFastArea, or on another lifecycle event) — inferred but not explicit.
The precise identity and lifetime of local2 (the TELEPAD object pointer) and whether TELEPAD::func0080 creates or merely initializes an existing telepad instance.
Whether multiple flags can be set in one run (e.g., overlapping map id ranges) or if exactly one flag is set per invocation in practice.

main_function.name: U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch
main_function.purpose: Record that the player has seen the teleporter on the current map (set the corresponding saw...Teleporter global flag). When active, attempt to start/initialize the telepad process and then suspend the U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch process.
entry_conditions: This is a process (processType 0x0210) associated with a teleporter U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch instance — invoked by the engine for the teleporter entity (exact trigger: instantiation or interaction/area entry is inferred).
Relies on the instance context ('this') so it runs for a specific teleporter object.

function_map.name: U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch
function_map.role: teleporter U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch process: set location-specific 'saw...Teleporter' flags and (inferred) initialize associated TELEPAD

function_map.name: Npc::getMap
function_map.role: query the map id for a given NPC/instance (used to determine which saw...Teleporter flag to set)

function_map.name: TELEPAD::func0080
function_map.role: telepad activation/initialization routine (invoked/spawned from U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch) — semantics inferred

function_map.name: spawn
function_map.role: engine primitive to start/spawn a separate process/function (used to run TELEPAD::func0080)

function_map.name: suspend
function_map.role: engine primitive to yield/suspend the current process (used after telepad activation attempt)

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the map id of the teleporter instance to decide which saw...Teleporter flag to set.
Initialize or activate the telepad associated with this U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch; the call is spawned so the telepad runs as a separate process (inferred from raw spawn usage).
Engine primitive used to run TELEPAD::func0080 as its own process; enables asynchronous telepad initialization (inferred).
Yield the U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch process after attempting telepad activation so other processes can run (common lifecycle behavior).

writes.name: sawCentralTenebraeTeleporter
writes.likely_effect: Marks that the player has seen the Central Tenebrae teleporter when the instance map id == 0x28.

writes.name: sawPlateauTeleporter
writes.likely_effect: Marks that the player has seen the Plateau teleporter when the instance map id == 0x08.

writes.name: sawUpperCatacombsTeleporter
writes.likely_effect: Marks that the player has seen the Upper Catacombs teleporter when the instance map id == 0x32.

writes.name: sawMountainKingTeleporter
writes.likely_effect: Marks that the player has seen the Mountain King teleporter when the instance map id == 0x1F.

writes.name: sawArgentrockIsleTeleporter
writes.likely_effect: Marks that the player has seen the Argentrock Isle teleporter when the instance map id == 0x25.

writes.name: sawCarthaxLakeTeleporter
writes.likely_effect: Marks that the player has seen the Carthax Lake teleporter when the instance map id == 0x10 or 0x1A.

writes.name: sawDaemonsCragTeleporter
writes.likely_effect: Marks that the player has seen the Daemon's Crag teleporter when the instance map id == 0x0C.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_TELPFLAG::hatch is a process that records that the player has 'seen' a teleporter by setting per-location saw...Teleporter global flags based on the instance's map id.
